everyone.name: Aakansha Priya
everyone.bio: Aakansha Priya is DevRel at EmpathyOps, a CNCF Ambassador and an organiser for KubeHuddle, Toronto. Working on empowering & upscaling the communities. In the past, she has been a Community Manager at Hashnode & WeMakeDevs, experienced in working efficiently in groups as well as individually.  As a frontend dev turned DevOps explorer, loves sharing tech insights, and views on learning & building in public, blogging & building personal brand. Aakansha, a reader and speaker, paints, sings, and loves spontaneous travel. She has spoken at various conferences about DevOps and the human side of tech. Catch her at meetups & conferences!

everyone.name: Rishit Dagli
everyone.bio: Rishit is a CS student at the University of Toronto. He spends most of his time researching and working with learning algorithms and vision. Currently, he also works as a research scientist intern at Qualcomm Research. He also maintains/contributes extensively to popular open-source projects like TensorFlow, PyTorch, Kubernetes, and Kubeflow among others and also frequently works on building new open-source projects. You will also often find him speaking at a CNCF conference.

everyone.name: Jon Zeolla
everyone.bio: Jon Zeolla is the founder of Zenable, where they are revolutionizing corporate governance by integrating cloud-native security and compliance with artificial intelligence. Previously he co-founded Seiso where he received the 2021 Start-Up Innovator of the Year Award and currently serves as strategic advisor, guiding key business decisions and company growth.He is also a CNCF Ambassador, SANS Instructor for SEC540: Cloud Security and DevSecOps Automation, and an active member of CNCF TAG Security.

everyone.name: Mandy Schoep
everyone.bio: Open source enthusiast with a passion for Linux, Kubernetes, automation, observability, and improving developer experience.She has worked with companies from a diverse range of industries, including banking, online retailing and semiconductors in roles such as Linux system administrator, DevOps engineer and web developer.

everyone.name: Hasan Alperen Selcuk
everyone.bio: I'm a DevOps and Cloud Engineer with a passion for managing Kubernetes clusters, no matter where they're hosted. I love sharing my knowledge through articles and videos about Kubernetes, helping others to understand and master it.  ☸️ Let's bring Kubernetes to every corner of the tech world! ☸️

everyone.name: Win Yan Naing Htut
everyone.bio: I have spent over 8 years evolving from traditional network engineering to building modern cloud-native systems. A huge fan of the CNCF landscape, I specialize in Kubernetes, DevSecOps, and Infrastructure as Code to solve complex challenges across multi-cloud environments. I love automating the heavy lifting to deliver resilient solutions, and you can see more of my work on my portfolio at https://portfolio.winyan.dev.

ambassadors.name: Aakansha Priya
ambassadors.bio: Aakansha Priya is DevRel at EmpathyOps, a CNCF Ambassador and an organiser for KubeHuddle, Toronto. Working on empowering & upscaling the communities. In the past, she has been a Community Manager at Hashnode & WeMakeDevs, experienced in working efficiently in groups as well as individually.  As a frontend dev turned DevOps explorer, loves sharing tech insights, and views on learning & building in public, blogging & building personal brand. Aakansha, a reader and speaker, paints, sings, and loves spontaneous travel. She has spoken at various conferences about DevOps and the human side of tech. Catch her at meetups & conferences!

ambassadors.name: Sascha Grunert
ambassadors.bio: Sascha works on many different container related open-source projects like Kubernetes and various container runtimes. He joined the CNCF community back in 2018, having gained container experience before. Sascha's passions include contributing to open source, as well as giving talks and evangelizing Kubernetes-related technologies.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to act as CNCF ambassador because mentoring and onboarding new people into the community is one of my passions! The CNCF ambassador program provides me a bigger context to connect with new and existing community members. It also makes it easier for me to boost the visibility of open source projects while evangelizing best practices, code of conduct and openness.

ambassadors.name: Rahul Jadhav
ambassadors.bio: An avid coder, a systems engineer working on solutions involving security and performance optimizations of cloud-native technologies. He runs a Kubernetes Cloud Native Security Bangalore Meetup and has been working closely with IETF Standards working groups. Taken several projects from conception to market and is an active maintainer for CNCF Sandbox project, "KubeArmor" and the lead author for IoT simulation framework "Whitefield". He co founded Accuknox where they are enabling runtime, zero-trust security by leveraging Linux-LSM/eBPF based security for k8s and containerized/cloud-native workloads.​Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Connecting to community and engaging at events, meeting new people, talking about cloud native technologies is something that I cherish. It is always great to be an ambassador of the community whose motto and modus operandi is something that I believe in. One specific goal that I would like to achieve is to establish a security focussed community locally by understanding activities that were undertaken at CNCF wide initiatives.

ambassadors.name: Sai Linn Thu
ambassadors.bio: Sai is currently working as Senior Solutions Architecture Specialist, APJ at HashiCorp and is based out of Singapore. Sai started his career in Networking and had 17 years of experience in private/public cloud infrastructure solutions for highly regulated security-conscious industries such as Banking, Financial Services Industries, Aviation, ISPs and Public Sector organizations across APJ Region. Previously He was part of the Field Engineering team at solo.io, Professional Services Consultant (APJ) at chef.io. He focuses on modernizing infrastructure automation and microservices application networking from Edge to Service Mesh at scale, where the tech stacks are built on HashiCorp Vault, Consul, Boundary, Terraform, Chef, Istio, Envoy Proxy, eBPF, Cilium, GraphQL built for Kubernetes, VMs & Cloud.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Singapore and ASEAN Region need more active and organized meetups around cloud native community. The activities are behind compare to US, EMEA and ANZ Region. As an ambassador, I will continue to create more awareness and advocate CNCF projects in Singapore and ASEAN Region. I am also participating for the KubeDay Singapore as one of the event chairs, and continue to contribute. I'm very proud of being CNCF Ambassador and representing CNCF in Singapore and APAC Region.

ambassadors.name: Dolis Sharma
ambassadors.bio: Dolis Sharma is a seasoned technologist with extensive experience guiding enterprises through their cloud-native journeys. She has deep expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, DevOps, and DevSecOps, helping organizations accelerate and scale cloud-native adoption. Dolis has built and managed mission-critical systems and successfully led multiple environments through rigorous security certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Known for translating complex technical challenges into tangible business outcomes, she partners closely with enterprise teams, offering architecture guidance, automation strategies, and operational best practices. When she’s not exploring the world of technology, she enjoys hiking, chasing waterfalls, relaxing on the beach, or dancing. She holds a diploma in Kathak and loves moving to the rhythm of the music

ambassadors.name: Andreas Grabner
ambassadors.bio: Been working in software since 1998 and from the early days was drawn to performance engineering, observability and more recently on DevOps, SRE, Platform Engineering. I am an active contributor to the CNCF project Keptn, I am a podcaster, I create YouTube tutorials and occasionally blog as well! Besides IT I am passionate about salsa dancing :-)Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The success of any community depends on the people that actively drive community activities (bringing people together, educating, making them feel comfortable ..) and the members of the community that then want to join, grow and eventually contribute back to that community. I am excited to get the chance to help grow the community with my goal to inspire many others to do the same

ambassadors.name: Jessica Andersson
ambassadors.bio: Jessica has extensive experience and expertise within cloud architecture, cloud infrastructure and platform engineering. She is a hands on, technical architect and team lead who has built up and led platform teams within developer experience and data. She is also a Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) ambassador who regularly shares her knowledge both in the local Gothenburg community and at conferences such as QCon.

ambassadors.name: Md Arif Hossen
ambassadors.bio: Arif is a Cloud, Platform, Cybersecurity, and AI Architect with over two decade of experience in telecom and cloud-native transformation. He is a passionate advocate for open source and community-driven innovation. As a community leader, Arif co-organizes community activity with Kubernetes User Group Bangladesh (K8SUGBD) and Cloud Native Rajshahi, fostering collaboration and knowledge-sharing across the region.

kubestronauts.name: Mandy Schoep
kubestronauts.bio: Open source enthusiast with a passion for Linux, Kubernetes, automation, observability, and improving developer experience.She has worked with companies from a diverse range of industries, including banking, online retailing and semiconductors in roles such as Linux system administrator, DevOps engineer and web developer.

kubestronauts.name: Chima Agwuncha
kubestronauts.bio: I am a self-motivated professional based in Budapest, with expertise in Telecoms, multi-Cloud, Cybersecurity, and IT. I hold certifications in Cloud, Linux and Kubernetes

kubestronauts.name: Abdullah Cosgun
kubestronauts.bio: Abdullah is a curious person who loves architecture and infrastructure. He is currently working as a DevOps Engineer at invent.ai. He enjoys travelling and playing video games on his free time.

kubestronauts.name: Alvaro Navarro
kubestronauts.bio: I’m a Platform Engineer from Chile with a strong focus on Kubernetes, observability, and DevSecOps. I’ve worked on projects involving Grafana, InfluxDB, Docker, and cloud infrastructure across GCP and Azure. I’ve contributed to initiatives that improve system monitoring, security, and deployment automation. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies and love sharing knowledge through meetups, talks, and hands-on workshops.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Ana Pedra
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cloud DevOps Engineer | Golden Kubestronaut 🚀 | Hackathon winner 🏆 and lifelong learner with 40+ certifications across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Red Hat, and Kubernetes. Passionate about cloud, Kubernetes, security, and automation — always learning, building, and giving back to the community.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Vikas Verma
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I specialize in Ruby on Rails and am passionate about cloud-native technologies. After just two months of learning Kubernetes, I built the entire infrastructure of a startup on managed Kubernetes and utilized other cloud-native technologies. I have contributed to the Rails framework and KubeArmor as part of the Linux Foundation mentorship program and created a couple of Ruby Gems.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Łukasz Amróz
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Frontend Developer that have been working with Kubernetes for the past three years. Interested in DevOps tools and technologies.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Rafael De Jesus Medeiros
goldenKubestronauts.bio: With more than ten years of experience in the IT field, I have a strong interest in cloud technologies, Kubernetes, Terraform and enhancing automation through coding. I like to help the community in my free time through my medium page. Feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn!
